codeReview.summary: Frontend wiring covers the new settings tab, navigation entry, beacon panel, and periodic sync context, but the implementation misses the required first-time setup UX and can silently disable remote-machine visibility when the backend stores a hostname that differs from the UI machine label.

issues.description: After enabling Sync Beacon without an existing Gist, the UI immediately switches to read-only 'created gist' mode whenever `config.syncBeaconGistId` becomes non-empty. That prevents users from entering a Gist ID to join another machine after first setup, even though the feature requires a persistent 'Gist ID input (for joining existing beacon)' alongside showing the created ID for sharing.

Remote-machine filtering uses the frontend's `syncBeaconMachineName || computerName || getHostname()` value to exclude the local machine, but the backend commit for this feature did not yet pass a machine name into `sync_beacon_push`. If the backend records a different hostname than the frontend fallback (for example OS hostname vs. saved computer name/'This Machine'), the local machine is not filtered out and appears in the remote machines panel, violating the requirement that the local machine is excluded from display.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture notes do not mention that Sync Beacon's self-filtering depends on the frontend and backend using the same machine identifier; this mismatch led to a later follow-up fix for machine-name propagation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/ui/src/lib/sync-beacon-context.tsx:91 filters by `syncBeaconMachineName || computerName || getHostname()`, while apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands.rs:1113 in the original backend command defaulted to its own hostname when no machine_name was provided. architecture.md has no Sync Beacon note about this coupling.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The tauri-fullstack skill tells workers to do browser verification, but for Tauri-dependent features like Sync Beacon that only produces a smoke check and misses behavioral issues in desktop command flows.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.deviations notes the worker only smoke-checked `/app` because gist interactions require Tauri runtime, and the missed local-machine filtering bug depended on the real desktop command path. .factory/skills/tauri-fullstack/SKILL.md does not call out this limitation except as generic browser verification guidance.

summary: Fail. Blocking issues: (1) the settings UI replaces the required join-by-Gist input with a read-only display once any Gist ID exists, so users cannot both view/share the created Gist and enter a different Gist to join another beacon later; (2) local-machine exclusion was unreliable because the frontend filtered using a different machine-name source than the backend push command used at this commit. No non-blocking code issues beyond those blockers.
